name: Run
description: A faster sustained movement state that makes traversal feel responsive.
design_notes: Run should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For movement mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use run as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect run to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels directly connected to the avatar and able to express intent through motion.

parameters.name: movement_speed
parameters.description: Baseline travel speed while the mechanic is active.

parameters.name: acceleration
parameters.description: How quickly the actor reaches target speed.

parameters.name: turn_response
parameters.description: How quickly movement direction updates after input changes.

edge_cases: Run is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Run overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Run runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Run state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Run plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Run produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Run strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Run becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Run with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Run as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Run with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Run as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
